title: Frank Is in a Coma

jokes.text: Oh, it tastes like cardboard.
jokes.setup: Characters discussing attention to detail and presentation of what seems like impressive cake
jokes.punchline: Reveal that despite impressive appearance, it tastes terrible
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective subversion - builds up fancy cake then deflates it immediately

dialogue.line: Oh, it tastes like cardboard.

jokes.text: Nobody gives a shit about cake, Frank.
jokes.punchline: Brutal dismissal of Frank's interests
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the gang's casual cruelty toward Frank with efficient delivery

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Nobody gives a shit about cake, Frank.

jokes.text: I threw it in the dumpster. - That's exactly where I got it.
jokes.punchline: Both characters got it from the dumpster
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - first one throws it away, second admits to dumpster diving, creates perfect absurd moment

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I threw it in the dumpster.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's exactly where I got it.

jokes.text: You going in the dumpsters these days? That's kinda my turf, no?
jokes.punchline: Territorial claim over dumpster diving rights
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - treating dumpster diving as a professional territory

dialogue.line: You going in the dumpsters these days? That's kinda my turf, no?

jokes.text: I mean, a bunch of rich assholes dressed to the nines, eating great food, talking about multimillion-dollar business deals. - That sounds awesome. - That sounds really fun.
jokes.setup: Character dismissing galas as waste of time with appealing description
jokes.punchline: Others immediately recognize it sounds great
jokes.explanation: Good irony where the character undermines their own argument by making galas sound appealing

dialogue.line: I mean, a bunch of rich assholes dressed to the nines, eating great food, talking about multimillion-dollar business deals.
That sounds awesome.
That sounds really fun.

jokes.text: That sounds awesome. / That sounds really fun.
jokes.setup: Frank's description of galas as terrible
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dennis immediately see the appeal
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Frank's ironic description. Simple but effective reaction comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That sounds awesome.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That sounds really fun.

jokes.text: You think we can't figure out what is or is not cake, Frank? That's so stupid.
jokes.setup: Character confidently dismissing cake identification as trivial
jokes.punchline: Sets up dramatic irony for later cake reveal
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's big reveal - gains value on rewatch when you know they literally can't tell

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: You think we can't figure out what is or is not cake, Frank? That's so stupid.

jokes.text: Why can't you just die and leave us your money, you know? Like every normal human being
jokes.punchline: Casual request for death framed as normal behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - treating death as normal parental duty, delivered casually

dialogue.line: Why can't you just die and leave us your money, you know? Like every normal human being

jokes.text: If it hurts, why you laughing?
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the contradiction in his reaction
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about tickling contradiction

dialogue.line: If it hurts, why you laughing?

jokes.text: Uh-oh.
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to Frank collapsing
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement for major medical emergency - very IASIP tone

dialogue.line: Uh-oh.

jokes.text: I've seen him go into a coma, like, every single night, and he always pops right up in the morning. - Well, that's just sleep. - You're describing sleep.
jokes.setup: Character trying to reassure about Frank's condition
jokes.punchline: Others point out he's just describing normal sleep
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic where character confuses basic sleep with medical coma

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I've seen him go into a coma, like, every single night, and he always pops right up in the morning.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, that's just sleep.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're describing sleep.

jokes.text: No, we could. We just get a big battery or something, like a Tesla battery. - No. That's so stupid. They don't make batteries that big. Gonna have to get a generator.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to wheel Frank around on life support
jokes.punchline: Serious discussion of powering life support equipment portably
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where they seriously problem-solve completely inappropriate scenario

dialogue.line: No, we could. We just get a big battery or something, like a Tesla battery.
No. That's so stupid. They don't make batteries that big. Gonna have to get a generator.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's me. What'd I win?
jokes.setup: Nurse asking if someone is Deandra
jokes.punchline: Dee treating medical responsibility as a prize
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's inappropriate optimism about serious medical situation

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's me. What'd I win?

jokes.text: Maybe because you tickled the man to death.
jokes.setup: Dee asking why she has to handle life support
jokes.punchline: Direct blame for the earlier tickling incident
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier scene with dark implication about cause of death

dialogue.line: Maybe because you tickled the man to death.

jokes.text: Where's the expression come from? 'Tickle a man to death.' - 'Tickled to death.' That's a thing.
jokes.punchline: Taking idiom literally in context of actual death
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting literal and figurative meanings of common expression

dialogue.line: Where's the expression come from? 'Tickle a man to death.' - 'Tickled to death.' That's a thing.

jokes.text: Well, there aren't chairs. There's a chair, and it's covered in wax. - I believe that's cheese. - I don't want to sit in cheese either.
jokes.punchline: Single disgusting chair covered in unidentifiable substance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where correction doesn't improve the disgusting situation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, there aren't chairs. There's a chair, and it's covered in wax.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I believe that's cheese.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't want to sit in cheese either.

jokes.text: Yeah. Like a Starbucks for dive bars.
jokes.punchline: Contradictory business concept combining upscale and downscale
jokes.explanation: Perfect oxymoronic business concept that captures the gang's delusional thinking

dialogue.line: Yeah. Like a Starbucks for dive bars.

jokes.text: schools, churches, like, little...
jokes.setup: Listing expansion locations for dive bars
jokes.punchline: Including completely inappropriate venues like schools and churches
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation showing complete lack of awareness about appropriate venues

dialogue.line: schools, churches, like, little...

jokes.text: And women ruin everything, we know that. - This woman does. - All women, kind of.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from specific to general misogyny
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate misogyny that escalates naturally, though predictable for these characters

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And women ruin everything, we know that.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This woman does.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All women, kind of.

jokes.text: It was like dude, dude, dude, dude, woman. Right?
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex business issues to gender sequence
jokes.explanation: Great Dennis pseudo-intellectual analysis reduced to absurd simplicity. Very quotable structure.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It was like dude, dude, dude, dude, woman. Right?

jokes.text: Headed down to South America. Gonna get some cosmetic surgery done on the cheap. I won't go into too much detail, but let's just say I'll probably need two seats on the flight back.
jokes.setup: Dee planning to use inheritance for surgery
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific implication about butt enhancement
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific vanity with creative euphemistic delivery

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Headed down to South America. Gonna get some cosmetic surgery done on the cheap. I won't go into too much detail, but let's just say I'll probably need two seats on the flight back.

jokes.text: I'm getting my ass done. Ooh. I might get a foot reduction while I'm down there too. Although, you know what? That's not a good idea. No, I'm gonna need these feet to carry that big fat ass around.
jokes.punchline: Logical reasoning about needing strong feet for enhanced butt
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic chain that's perfectly in character for Dee's vanity and poor decision-making

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm getting my ass done. Ooh. I might get a foot reduction while I'm down there too. Although, you know what? That's not a good idea. No, I'm gonna need these feet to carry that big fat ass around.

jokes.text: Why? - Well, we're, like, the Smuckers brothers. We're purveyors of fine jellies and jams from the Deep South.
jokes.setup: Proposing fake identities for the gala
jokes.punchline: Completely random jelly magnate backstory
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist choice of fake identity that's both specific and random

dialogue.line: Why?
Well, we're, like, the Smuckers brothers. We're purveyors of fine jellies and jams from the Deep South.

jokes.text: I think that you are confusing... The Smuckers brothers with the Smothers Brothers who were a folk singing duo from the '50s. - Shit. Yeah. - And they were, I think, Canadian.
jokes.punchline: Multiple layers of wrong information
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where the correction adds more confusion rather than clarity

dialogue.line: I think that you are confusing... The Smuckers brothers with the Smothers Brothers who were a folk singing duo from the '50s.
Shit. Yeah.
And they were, I think, Canadian.

jokes.text: I don't feel comfortable playing myself.
jokes.setup: Dennis suggesting they just be themselves
jokes.punchline: Charlie's discomfort with authenticity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about identity and performance. Charlie finds being himself harder than playing characters.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't feel comfortable playing myself.

jokes.text: I don't feel comfortable playing myself. We haven't done that in ages, man.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to just be themselves at the gala
jokes.punchline: Treating being yourself as an unfamiliar acting role
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about how these characters are always putting on acts, treating authenticity as performance

dialogue.line: I don't feel comfortable playing myself. We haven't done that in ages, man.

jokes.text: Mikey, they hate these Chardonnays. I need something nicer.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis at fancy gala
jokes.punchline: Being mistaken for waitstaff and playing along
jokes.explanation: Good situational comedy showing how they don't belong in high society

dialogue.line: Mikey, they hate these Chardonnays. I need something nicer.

jokes.text: You sound like you're on the Titanic.
jokes.punchline: Comparing phone quality to historical disaster
jokes.explanation: Good specific comparison for bad phone quality with dramatic historical reference

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You sound like you're on the Titanic.

jokes.text: Five bucks! Five bucks I found!
jokes.setup: Dee searching for valuable inheritance items
jokes.punchline: Extreme excitement over finding minimal money
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Dee's grand inheritance plans and pathetic reality

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Five bucks! Five bucks I found!

jokes.text: The tuxes definitely make us look like bathroom attendants.
jokes.punchline: Recognition that their formal wear makes them look like service staff
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness about their appearance and social status

dialogue.line: The tuxes definitely make us look like bathroom attendants.

jokes.text: A hundred percent. Like he locked us in a zoo and then now he's released us out into the Serengeti to be devoured.
jokes.setup: Blaming Frank for their social failures
jokes.punchline: Elaborate animal metaphor for their helplessness
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor that captures their victimization complex

dialogue.line: A hundred percent. Like he locked us in a zoo and then now he's released us out into the Serengeti to be devoured.

jokes.text: The tuxes definitely make us look like bathroom attendants.
jokes.setup: Being mistaken for service staff repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Dennis's realization about their formal wear backfiring
jokes.explanation: Good observational moment about how their attempt at class markers failed.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The tuxes definitely make us look like bathroom attendants.

jokes.text: I dismantle companies, sell the scraps for a marginal profit.
jokes.punchline: Casually describing predatory capitalism as his day job
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Simon's honest evil is refreshing compared to the gang's delusions.

dialogue.line: I dismantle companies, sell the scraps for a marginal profit.

jokes.text: No, no, I wasn't taking a shit. I was just... I was doing cocaine.
jokes.punchline: Cocaine use as preferable explanation to bathroom use
jokes.explanation: Great character introduction - priorities completely backwards. Very quotable.

dialogue.line: No, no, I wasn't taking a shit. I was just... I was doing cocaine.

jokes.text: No, no, I wasn't taking a shit. I was just... I was doing cocaine.
jokes.setup: Clarifying what he was doing in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Cocaine presented as the better explanation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where the 'clarification' makes things much worse

dialogue.line: No, no, I wasn't taking a shit. I was just... I was doing cocaine.

jokes.text: Did you hear what he said, 'soulless reptiles'? Pizzagate! - No, no. - Yeah. They're lizards!
jokes.punchline: Character immediately connecting to conspiracy theories
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how they misinterpret everything through conspiracy lens

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Did you hear what he said, 'soulless reptiles'? Pizzagate!
No, no.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah. They're lizards!

jokes.text: BigAssButts? 1973? Goddamn it!
jokes.punchline: Finding vintage pornography instead of valuables
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about Frank's predictable possessions vs. Dee's expectations

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: BigAssButts? 1973? Goddamn it!

jokes.text: I'll kick you a few CCs, and then you and me will take our brand-new dumpers and we'll just go, you know, flaunting around Copacabana Beach.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to bribe the nurse
jokes.punchline: Offering to share butt enhancement surgery as incentive
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier surgery talk with creative bribery attempt

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'll kick you a few CCs, and then you and me will take our brand-new dumpers and we'll just go, you know, flaunting around Copacabana Beach.

jokes.text: Frank, if you can hear me, I'm taking your big butt magazine! It's mine now!
jokes.punchline: Her petty theft as final revenge
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character moment. Dee's petty vindictiveness over worthless items.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Frank, if you can hear me, I'm taking your big butt magazine! It's mine now!

jokes.text: Crystal meth? - Oh. - Can you remind me what you guys, uh, do again?
jokes.punchline: Simon immediately becoming suspicious of their employment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where the drug revelation changes Simon's perception of them

dialogue.speaker: Simon
dialogue.line: Crystal meth?
Oh.

dialogue.speaker: Simon
dialogue.line: Can you remind me what you guys, uh, do again?

jokes.text: You like my money. - It's not... - You meet me in a bathroom, you do my drugs, and then I bleed green for you, for your fucking bar?
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis denying they want his money
jokes.punchline: Simon accurately describing their transparent opportunism
jokes.explanation: Great moment where someone actually calls out their obvious manipulation tactics

dialogue.speaker: Simon
dialogue.line: You like my money.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's not...

dialogue.speaker: Simon
dialogue.line: You meet me in a bathroom, you do my drugs, and then I bleed green for you, for your fucking bar?

jokes.text: I like you, guys! I would fucking love to give you money.
jokes.setup: Simon's angry rant about their opportunism
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal to enthusiastic support
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Simon's meth-induced emotional volatility and unpredictability.

dialogue.speaker: Simon
dialogue.line: I like you, guys! I would fucking love to give you money.

jokes.text: This was his most prized possession?
jokes.punchline: Discovering it's a crude child's drawing
jokes.explanation: Good emotional setup for the fake death reveal - shows Frank's actual sentiment

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: This was his most prized possession?

jokes.text: ♪ Follow the yellow brick road, my boys ♪
jokes.punchline: Singing while looking at apparently bizarre item
jokes.explanation: Setup for later revelation. Song choice suggests something Wizard of Oz related.

dialogue.line: ♪ Follow the yellow brick road, my boys ♪

jokes.explanation: Good dark foreshadowing. Password reveals the party's dangerous nature.

Narcan.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis

Narcan?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're in, boys.
All right.
We're in.

dialogue.line: Isn't that what they give you when you OD or something?
Yeah.

jokes.text: What's the password? - Narcan. - Well, that's a bit unsettling.
jokes.punchline: Password is drug overdose reversal medication
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy where the password reveals the party's true nature

dialogue.line: What's the password?
Narcan.
Well, that's a bit unsettling.

jokes.text: Take an idea and fuck me with it. - Okay, yeah. Sure... - Right now? - Well, we just have the... Don't overthink it, just fuck me. Fuck me with an idea.

jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation into absurd sexual business metaphors that creates maximum discomfort

dialogue.line: Take an idea and fuck me with it.
Okay, yeah. Sure...

dialogue.speaker: Simon

Well, we just have the...

jokes.text: Tastes sweet. - What do you mean? - Is he supposed to taste sweet? Shouldn't it be salty, if anything?
jokes.punchline: Immediate focus on unexpected flavor
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for the cake reveal - the sweetness is the first clue that builds suspense

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Tastes sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Is he supposed to taste sweet? Shouldn't it be salty, if anything?

jokes.text: Tastes sweet. - What do you mean? - Is he supposed to taste sweet? Shouldn't it be salty, if anything?
jokes.setup: Dee kissing seemingly dying Frank goodbye
jokes.punchline: Unexpected sweet taste instead of normal skin saltiness
jokes.explanation: Great setup for major reveal. Unexpected taste observation builds mystery.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Tastes sweet.
What do you mean?
Is he supposed to taste sweet? Shouldn't it be salty, if anything?

jokes.text: Cake. It's cake. - I'm cake. - What is happening?
jokes.setup: Entire episode building to Frank's death
jokes.punchline: Reveal that Frank and everything was elaborate cake setup
jokes.explanation: Brilliant episode-spanning setup and payoff. Frank's elaborate prank perfectly calls back to opening cake conversation and their confidence about identifying cake

dialogue.line: Cake. It's cake.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm cake.
What is happening?

jokes.text: I tricked you guys. You said you could tell the difference between what was cake and what was not cake, and you couldn't. Ha!
jokes.punchline: Frank explaining the elaborate prank motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the opening where they dismissed cake identification as trivial

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I tricked you guys. You said you could tell the difference between what was cake and what was not cake, and you couldn't. Ha!

jokes.text: I commissioned a cake. I rented all of this hospital equipment, and I even had to draw that little stupid drawing with you and me holding hands. "I love Daddy. I love you, Daddy."
jokes.punchline: Extent of preparation including fake sentimental drawing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Frank's commitment. Fake drawing detail shows his thoroughness.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I commissioned a cake. I rented all of this hospital equipment, and I even had to draw that little stupid drawing with you and me holding hands. "I love Daddy. I love you, Daddy."

jokes.text: You cried over a cake.
jokes.punchline: Reducing her genuine emotion to crying over dessert
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Frank's cruel reduction of Dee's rare genuine emotion

dialogue.line: You cried over a cake.

jokes.text: Like Starbucks for dive bars? - He gets it. Yes. - It's a terrible idea. I hate that idea. - It's a stupid idea, man. - That's a dumb, dumb idea.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis pitching their business idea to Frank
jokes.punchline: Frank's immediate and total dismissal of their 'brilliant' concept
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Frank's protective dismissal was actually sound business judgment

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Like Starbucks for dive bars?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He gets it. Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's a terrible idea. I hate that idea.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's a stupid idea, man.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's a dumb, dumb idea.

tmdb_overview: The five stages of grief set in for Dee as Frank lies on his deathbed. Meanwhile, the Guys plot their new path forward by pitching their business ventures to Philadelphia's elite.
